The fifth in this long running submarine sim and without doubt the worst version. It has taken the very best of the third and fourth iterations and removed them from the game. Avoid.

If you want to waste $14.95 on a nine year old buggy mess, be my guest. If you want to transform the game into something worth playing by installing The Wolves of Steel megamod, don't bother. Ubisoft have recently acknowledged (after nine years) that these confounded folk have no right to mess around with SH5 and have taken steps to eradicate this despicable practice.

[EDIT]: July 2021
I'm reliably informed that the Wolves of Steel modded Steam version of this game will run with the Ubisoft launcher - so I have changed my recommendation.

YOU NEED TWOS FOR IT TO BE GREAT!

So, Just to preface my positive review, [b]you MUST get the Wolves of Steel mega-modset for this game off of the Subsim(dot)com for it to work right.[/b] A quick google search will direct you to the forum to find it.

With the modset, the game is amazing. Manually inputting info into the TDC, customizing the game-play to the level of realism you want (within reason), and sinking tonnage. There is nothing more satisfying than sneaking a volley of torpedoes through a destroyer screen to sink a Queen Elizabeth-class Battleship, and get away with it, or hit a 7km torpedo shot because you did the proper calculations and they never even knew you were there. (personal experiences, yours may vary) There are still a few bugs here and there in the modset, but once you recognize them, a quick save and reload will fix them up. 
In the Wolves of Steel modset, there are historical events added in via received radio messages, which actually happened during the war, such as Operation Wikinger, (you can look it up) or during Operation Weserübung, which is an in-game mission, but had a lot happen during the operation to take over Norway, while you are tasked to sink tonnage in the area.

Without the mod-set, well... the missions don't really work, you can still manually fire the torpedoes and get the same shots described above to hit with the manual TDC input, but there is no guarantee it will count for your objective in the area. There are "tonnage bar" quotas to certain missions, but rather than a certain class of tonnage to be sunk, like "merchant tonnage," the game uses certain ship files for what needs to be sunk,("Merchant ship_124" or something like that) which are only spawned in specific groups, so you could sink 2 large freighters for 7k tonnes each, for your 20k tonne quota, but neither will count because they weren't the right ship for the game to recognize, or weren't flying the right flag.

Ubisoft pushed this game out in a complete mess, like a lot of their other games from the time period that are notorious for being buggy, because at the time, they realized that Assassin's Creed was what was making them money. They dropped everything they couldn't fit to their new AC formula, which is easily recognizable as, "Find tall tower, reveal area, activities and collectibles, do activities and pickup collectibles." Said formula was successful enough that other companies copied it. *cough cough Horizon*( I loved HZD btw, story was awesome, no hate)
After that, it was up to modders to support and fix. And they really did. With mods from Subsim, this game is still pretty relevant. Without them, you are better of buying one of the other U-boat games out there. But all mods are free, unless you wish to donate to the modders, if they are still doing that, that is.

That being said, i would recommend at least starting a game without the WoS mod just so you can experience the beginning storyline of being promoted from First Officer to Captain of the boat. Otherwise if you don't care about that, start with the mod and make up your own story(in your head) about how you were given command of the boat.

$10 for this game is pushing it for its released version, so if you can get it cheaper, that would be better. This game is sadly one that REQUIRES mods to get your moneys worth out of and actually get to work right.

I have no experience with SH3 or SH4, so this is truly a review based on this game and genre. I have heard both are better than this in terms of gameplay, but are limited in the fact that you can not walk around the sub. Rather, you are restricted to switching between stations. 

Side note, it does require having a Uplay account and it seems people are having difficulty with updates due to dropped support. I can't hold the same claim, but i also have had the game for a very long time, so take that as you will.

2021 review for the "Wolves of Steel" modded version of the game: Soon after installation I was googling  "how do ships navigate at sea?" "How do I use a sextant?", "how did the ENIGMA machine work?" Yes, with mods this game includes an option to decode encrypted messages using a working Enigma machine and the option to navigate manually using a working sextant and other navigational tools. Basically, if you get this game, you have to download the amazing WOS modpack which comes with its own installer for steam and very specific instructions that are easy to follow. Be warned as the mod can make certain aspects *very* realistic - even playing other sub sims did not prepare me for some of the available options. 

Thankfully, the difficulty can be adjusted by turning on/off certain optional mods that come with the Wolves of Steel mod set so you dont have to learn everything at once. The modpack adds a tonnnnnnnnn of features and it makes me feel like I am playing an MMO - it really does feel like there's SO MUCH going on and you are only one small part of a much larger war. Example; first attempt at sinking an enemy destroyer was aided by the fact it had been damaged by a squadron of friendly divebombers I saw returning to base (thanks for the help little guys!). Docks are massive facilities inside of living port cities with ships coming and going, the occasional recon plane flying overhead... very immersive game. Despite its age, the mod makes this game look pretty incredible at times - the way light interacts with smoke and mist during night attacks? Havent seen another game do that as well. It's quite pleasant to hang out on the conning tower and watch the waves. 

Given the years of work that went into the mod, the game is well worth the money if you are looking to get into sub sims. Very satisfying game to learn and think about; it's a great feeling when everything starts to make sense.

Despite the criminal state Ubisoft left this game in, with a bit of time, patience and modding (The Wolves of Steel Megamod to be specific), this game is honestly great.

Example of bugs encountered during the first few hours of game play:
-Climbing up outside of sub only to find that it's actually underwater with some of the crew at surface stations as if nothing was wrong.  
-Mouse getting stuck in periscope view.
-Depth check button doesn't work.
-Damaging a ship and then loading a game to find that's now totally fine and undamaged.
-Looking through the periscope underwater doesn't show undersides of ships despite optional torpedo camera showing them but then again saw a ship go under the waves, half submerged.  Looked cool but then looked again and the underwater part of the ship had totally disappeared.  Meanwhile, the ship was still sending out sonar pulses as if nothing happened despite only its rear end sticking out of the water.
-CO2 levels kept rising despite surfacing and when surfaced, none of the crew manned their usual surface stations for some reason.  Guess they preferred more CO2 over fresh air despite yelling for me to surface.  Eventually, everyone died for a Game Over.
